user_description:
TVT: Suggestion - Document the mininum recommended screen resolution for Workbench

Our testers have found that 640x480 is not enough to display some dialogs; ; e.g.; the import/export dialogs have fields that are inaccessible in the lower ; resolutions (and the dialog is not resizable).; ; If 800x600 was the intended minimum; it should be stated somewhere in the ; programmer documentation.  FWIW; Windows seems to stick by the 640x480 minimum ; (or at least I was unable to find an exception).

[Editor Mgmt] Add new external fil editor does not preserve icon

20011107; ; If I add a new file editor teh icon doesnt show up; ; Windows->preferences; Workbench->File editor; Select *.xml; click add assciated editor; seelct external programs; IMPORTANT: click browse and select an executable with a different icon; do not chose the one in teh list; ; if you chose the one in teh list; teh icon will come with; if you select the ; same executable using browse; the icon will not; ; used to work in previous builds

Synchronize with stream always throws exception

Build 20011105; ; It is not possible to use VCM (CVS) with that build.; ; 1. Select a project; 2. From its context menu: Team -> Synchronize; ; ==> ; ; Log: Tue Nov 06 09:04:22 GMT+01:00 2001; 4 org.eclipse.vcm.ui 1 Internal error; java.lang.Error: Unresolved compilation problem: ; 	PServerConnection cannot ; be resolved or is not a type; ; 	at java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstance(Native Method); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.core.ccvs.client.PServerConnectionMethod.createConnecti; on(PServerConnectionMethod.java:21); itoryLocation.java:202); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.core.ccvs.client.Client.<init>(Client.java:229); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.core.ccvs.CVSAdapter.internalFetchRemoteTree(CVSAdapter; .java:761); 	at ; org.eclipse.core.internal.resources.Workspace.run(Workspace.java:1184); 	at org.eclipse.vcm.internal.core.Subscriber.run(Subscriber.java:627); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.core.SharingManager.run(SharingManager.java:772); a:78); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.ui.sync.Repository.localServerDelta(Repository.java:256; ); 	at ; org.eclipse.vcm.internal.ui.sync.SyncCompareInput$4.run(SyncCompareInput.java:47; 1); 	at ; org.eclipse.core.internal.resources.Workspace.run(Workspace.java:1184); a:98)

VCM leaves inconsistent resources

When switching to use a new teamstream (INTEGRATION) for my project;I first ; connected my project to this new teamstream; and added its content (of course ; empty since new) to the workspace.; ; It did remove all my local content for the project; but the binary output.; When attempting to add a different version of my project; it did fail because of ; an inconsistent resource.; ; The only workaround is to force a refresh from local on the output folder prior ; to adding the project version.

Support to remove a change from the list

SWT request:; support to remove/delete a change from a list/tree. SWT asks for it since they ; want to have a way to say they are done with this change.

TVT text clipped.  Korean text is clipped on

Korean text is clipped on text compare.  The top of the text is clipped off. ; ; To repro;reference TVT Case 10.1 ; http://jumpstart.raleigh.ibm.com/jumpstart/TVTDOCS/eclipsewbtvt.htm#_Toc52795487; 6

Allow importing of a project from the local file system

At the moment there is no convenient way to add an existing; project to the workspace.; This is particularly useful where your project is kept in a ; source control system not supported by Eclipse or the project; needs to be distributed in some other form.

ESC on rename dialog renames the file

Build: 20011105; ; 1. Open Navigator view; 2. Select a file; 3. Context menu --> Rename; 4. Enter a new name; 5. Press ESC; ==> instead of aborting the operation the file is renamed.; ; Note: this one is not maje; ; Could be related: http://dev.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=2934

Persist show version info

The summary says it all. We should persist the Show Version Info option that; appears on the Navigator in the Team Perspective.

Cant save files when a network address is not assigned

Under Linux; if the machine is connected to a network; but a DNS; query for its name does not return an address; then the editor; will not save files.; ; An example of how this can occur:; - A laptop machine is configured to use DDNS on a corporate network.; - When connected to a network outside the corporate firewall;;   a DNS server will not correctly resolve its name.; ; A temporary workaround for this problem is to place an entry; in /etc/hosts for the machines name; assigning it to the; same address as localhost. However; doing this will cause; other system services (mail servers; etc.) to work incorrectly.; ; When this occurs; saving a file generates the following error in; the log window:; ; ; ; java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: ; org/eclipse/core/internal/utils/UniversalUniqueIdentifier; 	at ; org.eclipse.core.internal.localstore.BlobStore.addBlob(BlobStore.java:26); 	at ; org.eclipse.core.internal.localstore.HistoryStore.addState(HistoryStore.java:107);
